techniques.evidence_quote: turning off JavaScript or using an ad-blocker could help thwart the code
hooking visitors' computers into a virtual currency mining operation

attack_chain.description: Attacker gained access to PolitiFact's website via a misconfigured cloud computing server, possibly through a third-party ad provider.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The issue may be related to a third-party ad provider

evidence_chunks.quote: The issue may be related to a third-party ad provider
The issue may be related to a third-party ad provider

attack_chain.description: Attacker injected Coin Hive JavaScript code into the PolitiFact website, programmed to run multiple instances simultaneously.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: somebody has programmed the site to run multiple versions of Coin Hive simultaneously

evidence_chunks.quote: somebody has programmed the site to run multiple versions of Coin Hive simultaneously
somebody has programmed the site to run multiple versions of Coin Hive simultaneously

attack_chain.description: Coin Hive script hijacked CPU resources of site visitors to mine Monero cryptocurrency, maxing out their CPUs.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: visiting Politifact.com caused his computer's CPU to run at its maximum capacity

evidence_chunks.quote: visiting Politifact.com caused his computer's CPU to run at its maximum capacity
visiting Politifact.com caused his computer's CPU to run at its maximum capacity

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured cloud computing server, possibly via a third-party ad provider, enabled injection of malicious JavaScript into PolitiFact.com
persistence_mechanism: Malicious Coin Hive JavaScript embedded in site content until discovered and removed
